summary: Iteration 176: Verified Times New Roman formatting across case detail tabs and print/export views. All 9 grounds for case_ac67738895f9 (R v Marika) have appellate pathways populated. Case identity card uses 14pt Times New Roman for values. Print views use 12pt body text with proper heading hierarchy. Footer includes document name, appellant name, and date.
critical_code_review_comments: Case identity card values (Defendant, Offence, State, Sentence) use Times New Roman 14pt as specified
All 9 grounds have appellate_pathway field populated with relevant legal references
Grounds screen view displays Appellate Pathway section in blue box for each ground
Grounds Print View uses Times New Roman 12pt body, 22pt h1, 18pt h2, 14pt h3
exportHtml.js shared template uses Times New Roman with 10pt italic footer
Footer format: 'Criminal Appeal Case Management - {Section} on {Appellant} - {Date}'
Print All button in header opens Complete Case Bundle view
All 9 tabs (Documents, Timeline, Grounds, Notes, Reports, Legal, Case Law, Progress, Collaboration) navigate correctly
Timeline print HTML uses Times New Roman 12pt body with proper footer
WebSocket connection warning for chat is non-critical (chat feature works)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 176 verified Times New Roman formatting for legal documents. Test case: case_ac67738895f9 (R v Marika, NT). Key findings: (1) Case identity card values use 14pt Times New Roman, (2) All 9 grounds have appellate_pathway populated, (3) Grounds print view uses 12pt body/22pt h1/18pt h2/14pt h3, (4) Footer includes document name, appellant, date, (5) Print All button works and shows Complete Case Bundle, (6) All 9 tabs navigate correctly, (7) exportHtml.js uses Times New Roman throughout.
